$description: ARIEL-only extension tokens: relevance-score badge colors (ariel-score-*). Source: ariel/static/css/variables.css lines 56-61 (dark) and 184-189 (light). ARIEL's own --color-amber/--font-sans etc. are NOT reproduced here — those are retired in Task 3.5 in favor of the canonical amber.*/font.display tokens; only the score-badge triplet is a genuine ARIEL-specific extension. Every score.* value here already coincides exactly with an existing ramp step used elsewhere (no new primitives needed). The high-contrast-dark/high-contrast-light groups are AUTHORED explicitly (not opted out) because these badge foreground colors are genuine information-bearing text, and checking them against the high-contrast family's own (more extreme) bg.primary values via validate.py's contrast_ratio() surfaced two real gaps the base dark/light values don't cover: (1) dark's 'low' (slate.650) only reaches 4.31:1 against high-contrast-dark's near-black bg — bumped to slate.600 (7.22:1); (2) light's 'high'/'medium' (green.700/amber.600) only reach ~3.1:1 against high-contrast-light's near-white bg (below even AA) — bumped to green.800/amber.900 (4.84:1 / 6.40:1). The *-bg tint composites are unchanged (decorative low-alpha backgrounds, not text).

low.$description: Bumped from dark's slate.650 (4.31:1 vs high-contrast-dark's bg.primary) to slate.600 (7.22:1).

high.$description: Bumped from light's green.700 (~3.18:1 vs high-contrast-light's bg.primary) to green.800 (4.84:1).

medium.$description: Bumped from light's amber.600 (~3.08:1 vs high-contrast-light's bg.primary) to amber.900 (6.40:1).
